risk_domain.name: Governance Proposal Risk Analysis
risk_domain.description: Detects malicious or high-risk governance proposals before execution, covering treasury drains, parameter manipulation, proxy upgrades, timelock bypasses, and voter concentration

capabilities: submit governance proposals with arbitrary calldata
accumulate or borrow voting power (flash loans, token purchases)
coordinate votes across multiple controlled addresses
front-run timelock execution with additional proposals
limitations: no private key access to treasury multisigs
cannot bypass on-chain timelock without a governance vote

H1_treasury_drain_pattern.name: treasury drain pattern
H1_treasury_drain_pattern.description: Series of transactions that consistently target treasury balances and may indicate a coordinated attack.

signals.name: treasury_drain_large_value
signals.description: Transaction value exceeds a certain percentage of the current treasury balance.

signals.name: treasury_drain_frequent_sends
signals.description: Multiple transactions to a single address with no known label within a short period.

signals.name: treasury_drain_multiple_tokens
signals.description: Transfer of multiple token types in a single transaction, consistent with a treasury drain pattern.

recommendations.description: Monitor treasury balance for any unusual activity.
Use smart contracts to restrict transfer permissions.
Configure alerts for transactions exceeding a certain threshold.
Block access to treasury from suspicious addresses.

H2_parameter_anomaly_detection.name: parameter anomaly detection
H2_parameter_anomaly_detection.description: Unusual parameter changes outside typical historical ranges or without proper simulations, potentially risky upgrades.

signals.name: parameter_outside_std_dev
signals.description: Proposed parameter change deviates significantly from historical average and standard deviation.

signals.name: unusual_proposal_count
signals.description: Number of proposed changes in a short period is unusually high or low compared to historical data.

signals.name: suspicious_recipient
signals.description: Recipient address has an unusual number of transactions and lacks known labels or affiliations.

signals.name: multiple_token_types_transfer
signals.description: Transfer involves multiple token types, potentially indicative of sweeping treasury funds.

recommendations.description: Manually verify the need for parameter changes.
Set up alerts for unusual proposal counts.
Automate the labeling of recipient addresses to avoid suspicious activity.
Review transfers involving multiple token types for legitimacy.
Implement AI to automatically detect and mitigate parameter anomalies.

H3_proxy_upgrade_risk.name: proxy upgrade risk
H3_proxy_upgrade_risk.description: Upgrades to unverified contracts with significant risks like self-destruct or lack of review time.

signals.name: proxy_upgrade_unverified
signals.description: Detected calls to unverified contracts or smart contracts with suspicious self-destruct patterns.

signals.name: proxy_upgrade_time_window
signals.description: Transaction occurs shortly after contract upgrade, suggesting a rushed deployment lacking proper review time.

recommendations.description: Manually verify the contract code before upgrade.
Wait for a predefined period after an upgrade before interacting with the new contract.
Automatically audit the upgraded contract using security tools like Slither or MythX.
Implement a policy requiring a minimum time delay between contract upgrades and usage.

H4_timelock_evasion.name: timelock evasion
H4_timelock_evasion.description: Attempts to bypass timelocks through changes in delays, administrators, or emergency functions.

signals.name: timelock_bypass_attempt
signals.description: Unusual changes in timelock duration or timing patterns, especially during critical governance periods.

signals.name: alternate_admin_activation
signals.description: Activation of secondary admin functions or accounts outside normal operational procedures.

signals.name: emergency_function_abuse
signals.description: Use of emergency functions or bypass mechanisms beyond predefined conditions.

recommendations.description: Set up continuous monitoring and logging for all timelock-related activities.
Periodically audit admin accounts to detect any unauthorized changes.
Enforce strict timelock rules and review them regularly.
Deploy a security orchestration tool to automate response to timelock bypass attempts.

H5_voter_concentration_risk.name: voter concentration risk
H5_voter_concentration_risk.description: High centralization of voting power among a few entities, potentially compromising governance.

signals.name: voter_concentration
signals.description: Identify instances where a small number of entities control a disproportionately large share of voting power.

signals.name: voter_activity
signals.description: Detect entities that have recently increased their voting activity significantly, potentially signaling centralization.

signals.name: voting_power_drift
signals.description: Monitor changes in the distribution of voting power over time to identify sudden shifts that might indicate centralization.

signals.name: high_voter_turnout
signals.description: Assess if a small number of entities consistently make up the majority of votes cast, suggesting centralization.

recommendations.description: Set thresholds for minimum voting power to participate in critical decisions.
Automatically distribute voting power randomly among users to prevent centralization.
Regularly rotate which entities have the right to vote on certain issues.
Periodically review and report voter concentration levels to ensure fairness.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring detection accuracy across all governance attack vectors

methodology.step_1: Collect historical governance proposals from Compound, Aave, Uniswap, Tornado Cash, Beanstalk (N >= 500)
methodology.step_2: Label proposals as benign, suspicious, or confirmed-attack
methodology.step_3: Run all 5 heuristics against each proposal
methodology.step_4: Measure per-heuristic precision, recall, and F1
methodology.step_5: Measure combined detection rate and false alarm rate
methodology.step_6: Test recommendations by simulating countermeasure application

combined_benchmark.target: detect 100% of known governance attacks with <5% false alarm rate on benign proposals

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including proxy upgrade analysis

templates.risk_assessment_block: BLOCKED: Proposal {proposal_id} flagged by {heuristic_name} with {confidence}% confidence. {signal_description}. This proposal poses unacceptable risk.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_proposals} governance proposals. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_proposals} proposals pass security checks. No governance attack indicators detected.
templates.skill_suggestion: To evaluate {heuristic_name} risk, I can {skill_action}. This provides {effectiveness_pct}% detection confidence. {user_cost_note}
